summary: Second cleanup pass over item headers and item-related declarations. The PR deleted `src/melee/it/inlines.h`, moved or kept item helper functions in `item.c`, expanded `item.h`/`types.h` declarations and field names, renamed item-related symbols such as `func_8026B1A4` to `Item_IsGrabbable`, and adjusted item struct field names such as `xD60_destroyType` to `destroy_type`, `xD7C_destroySFX` to `destroy_sfx`, and SFX sentinel fields to `sfx_unk1`/`sfx_unk2`. It also added Doxygen grouping/private annotations, updated includes, and made small related naming fixes in fighter/audio headers. Review feedback specifically pushed against putting local helpers in `it/inlines.h`; the author removed that header and acknowledged the helper placement issue.
what_changed: Deleted `src/melee/it/inlines.h`, which previously only contained `GetItemData` and `GetItemDirect` wrappers around `item_gobj->user_data`.
Kept item helper functions in `item.c` rather than a separate inline header, including helpers such as `HSD_JObjSetScaleItem`, `HSD_JObjSetFacingDirItem`, and `ItUnkHoldKind`.
Converted many formerly `static` item-local functions in `item.c` into non-static definitions with `/// @private` annotations, matching the PR author's note about Doxygen limitations.
Added Doxygen group annotation for the item module with `/// @addtogroup melee_it_item item` in `item.c`.
Renamed the item allocator variable from `unkAllocData0` to `itemAllocData`, preserving `unkAllocData1` for dynamic bone allocation.
Updated object allocation/free wiring to use `itemAllocData` and renamed the user-data removal callback use from `lbl_8026AE10` to `Item_OnUserDataRemove` in the shown diff.
Renamed `func_8026B1A4` callsites in fighter assembly comments/symbol references to `Item_IsGrabbable`.
Renamed an SFX local in `func_80087D0C` from `sfx_id` to `sfx`, including related comments for `Player_AdjustSFXIDForSizeModifier`.

Renamed item fields in `item.c` from offset-style names to semantic names where evidence existed: `xD60_destroyType` became `destroy_type`, `xD7C_destroySFX` became `destroy_sfx`, and `xD64`/`xD68` became `sfx_unk1`/`sfx_unk2` initialized to `SFX_NONE`.
Adjusted `code_80266F3C.h` declaration for `lbl_804D6D14` from `u32` to `uint`, with a note that it is probably `size_t` but blocked by current type definitions.

Added item/audio/library includes to support the moved declarations and renamed constants, including `melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h`, `sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h`, and `sysdolphin/baselib/gobjplink.h` in the shown `item.c` diff.
smart_moves: Removed a tiny inline header instead of spreading item internals across another file; this reduced header surface area and matched reviewer guidance that the helpers could stay in `item.c`.
Used semantic field names only where behavior was visible in the code: destroy reason/type fields were renamed around item cleanup paths, and SFX fields were tied to `SFX_NONE`/destroy SFX usage.
Preserved uncertainty explicitly with `@todo` comments, such as noting `lbl_804D6D14` is probably `size_t` but cannot yet be expressed that way cleanly.
Updated assembly symbol comments/calls to use the newly understood `Item_IsGrabbable` name, improving cross-system readability for fighter code that queries item grabbability.
Applied `/// @private` annotations to functions that needed declarations/documentation without implying they are public API, addressing Doxygen constraints noted in the PR body.
decomp_lessons: Do not create or keep an `inlines.h` just for trivial `HSD_GObj` user-data accessors or local item helpers; if the helpers are implementation details and not broadly reused, keep them in the `.c` file.
When Doxygen cannot mark a function private from the header, annotate the definition with `/// @private` rather than distorting code organization.

Semantic struct field names should be driven by repeated usage patterns. In this PR, `destroy_type` is supported by multiple item destruction paths such as animation end, lifetime expiry, blast zone checks, collision, and jumped-on handling.
Renaming an unknown function to a domain name should be propagated into assembly references and dependent systems so search and future matching work use the new symbol consistently.
If a suspected type improvement is blocked by project-wide typedef choices, document the reason with `@todo` instead of silently choosing an over-specific or possibly wrong type.

Reviewer feedback favored minimizing exported/header-visible implementation details; future item cleanup should separate actual API from functions that merely need declarations for matching or Doxygen.
naming_conventions: `func_8026B1A4` was renamed to `Item_IsGrabbable`, using the `Item_` prefix plus a predicate-style verb phrase.
`unkAllocData0` was renamed to `itemAllocData`, tying allocator globals to the object type they allocate.
`xD60_destroyType` was renamed to `destroy_type`, dropping the offset prefix once the field's purpose was clear.
`xD7C_destroySFX`/item attribute `x78_destroySFX` were represented as `destroy_sfx`, using lowercase snake_case for struct fields.
`xD64` and `xD68` became `sfx_unk1` and `sfx_unk2`, a partial semantic name that records the SFX domain while preserving uncertainty.
The fighter local variable `sfx_id` was shortened to `sfx`; the diff shows this as a readability cleanup, not a newly proven type distinction.
`lbl_8026AE10` was referenced as `Item_OnUserDataRemove` at the shown callsite, following an event/callback-style name for a GObj user-data destructor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly references in `asm/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.s` were updated from `func_8026B1A4` to `Item_IsGrabbable` without changing instruction bytes, indicating a symbol/name-only improvement useful for non-decompiled callsites.

`HSD_JObjSetFacingDirItem` was written with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH inline` in the review excerpt, showing the common tactic of conditional inline control for matching while preserving a normal C definition.
`func_8026814C` and `lbl_80269528` retained `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding in the excerpt, showing that header/visibility cleanup was done without discarding existing matching shims.
The deleted `inlines.h` accessors returned `item_gobj->user_data`; future matching should avoid hiding such direct accesses unless an inline is demonstrably needed for codegen.
Renaming fields like `destroy_type` did not change logic in the shown diff, making the PR primarily a symbol/type/header cleanup rather than a behavioral rewrite.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: these can all stay in item.c
review_feedback.lesson: Implementation-only item helpers should not be moved into a separate inline header without a concrete reuse or matching reason.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: I was hoping you wouldn't notice so I could move them later when I'm not being lazy but ok
review_feedback.lesson: The author accepted the review direction and removed the inline-header approach.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: I think this might be in `lb` or something because `Fighter` and `Item` both have their `facing_dir` at 0x2C.
review_feedback.lesson: Shared helper candidates should be identified cautiously; similar field offsets between `Fighter` and `Item` suggested a possible lower-level/library helper, but the evidence was not conclusive.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: There are some weird Doxygen comments in this one because I still haven't fully figured out how certain things work... you can't mark a function `@private` from the header, it must be marked at the definition.
review_feedback.lesson: Some unusual Doxygen placement was intentional tooling workaround rather than a claim of finished documentation design.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename `sfx_unk1` or `sfx_unk2` to determine their final semantics.
Search for later movement of `HSD_JObjSetFacingDirItem` into `lb` or another shared library, as the author suspected it might be shared with Fighter due to `facing_dir` at offset 0x2C.
Check subsequent item header PRs for whether `lbl_804D6D14` was eventually changed to `size_t` or whether the typedef issue was resolved.
Search for later cleanup of functions marked `/// @private` in `item.c` to see which became public declarations and which stayed implementation-only.
Compare later `item.h` and `types.h` revisions for continued field renames around `destroy_type`, `destroy_sfx`, and item allocation/user-data lifecycle.
